campaigns.title: SVX — Webinar Re-engagement
campaigns.webinarAngle: Exclusivity — SVX investor audience gets early access to the Climate Tech Exit Lab framework before it goes public. BTC brings the content, SVX brings the list.
campaigns.waitingOn: Bryan's approval — Keyona sent Bryan the proposed email to James asking what he thinks before it goes out. Bryan has not sent yet.
campaigns.usageNote: Keyona proposed this re-engagement email to Bryan for his sign-off. Contact is James at SVX. Once Bryan approves the draft, Bryan sends it. Status should move to 'needs_send' once Bryan confirms, then 'waiting' after it's sent to James.
campaigns.emailSubject: Picking back up — webinar idea for your community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey James,

Hope the new year is treating you well. We connected late last year and I've been thinking about a collaboration that could be a good fit for your community.

We're putting together a webinar series on climate tech exits — breaking down how exits actually work in this space for small-check investors who want to understand the landscape before they commit capital. It's practical, framework-driven content that honestly doesn't exist anywhere else right now.

I'd love to co-host one session with SVX. You bring the audience, we bring the content. Low lift on your end, real value for your community.

Worth a 20-minute call to see if it makes sense?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Ontario Clean Tech Industry Association — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Peer credibility — not an investor lecture, a practitioner conversation. BTC as people who understand the founder and operator journey in climate tech. Frame as real talk, not a pitch.
campaigns.usageNote: Bryan's direct network. First touch. Lead with value to their members — founders and operators — not BTC's brand or credentials.
campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar idea for your members — climate tech exits
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey [Name],

I'm Bryan — I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC focused on the earliest stages of the ecosystem.

I've been building a webinar series aimed at climate tech founders and operators, and I think your community at OCTIA would be a great fit for a session.

The content is practitioner-focused: how exits actually work in climate tech, what the different paths look like, and what founders and operators need to understand before they get there. It's the kind of conversation that doesn't happen enough in this space — too much noise, not enough ground truth.

I'd love to co-host a session with you. You bring the members, we bring the framework. Fully co-branded, no pitch, just useful content.

Open to a quick call to see if it's a fit?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Clean Tech North — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Regional investment — BTC is building with the Canadian ecosystem, not passing through. The webinar is a proof point of that. Co-branding signals both orgs are invested in the region.
campaigns.usageNote: Bryan's direct network. Regional identity is the lever. Lead with commitment to the Canadian climate tech community, not a generic webinar pitch.
campaigns.emailSubject: Building something for the Canadian climate tech community — want to co-host?
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey [Name],

I'm Bryan — I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC. I've been intentional about building with the Canadian ecosystem, not just showing up when it's convenient.

Part of that is a webinar series I'm putting together on climate tech exits — practical content on how exits actually work in this space, built for founders and investors who are in the thick of it. Not a pitch, not a panel. Real frameworks from people who've been close to these deals.

Clean Tech North has the regional reach and the right community for this. I'd love to co-host a session with you — you bring the audience, we bring the content. Fully co-branded.

Would love to connect and see if there's something here.

Bryan

campaigns.title: KAFD — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Credibility amplification — co-branding with BTC signals to their network they are plugged into real emerging fund managers doing climate work. Mutual visibility play.
campaigns.blocker: Need full org name and right contact from Bryan before sending. Draft is ready — just needs [Org Full Name] and [Name] filled in.
campaigns.usageNote: KAFD full name TBC. Draft is written and ready to go the moment Bryan confirms the org name and who to address it to.

Hey [Name],

I'm Bryan — I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC, and I've been putting together a webinar series on climate tech exits.

The content is built for communities at the intersection of climate and capital — practical frameworks on how exits actually work in this space, what the different paths look like, and what investors need to understand before they get into these deals.

I think [Org Full Name] and BTC would be a natural fit for a co-hosted session. You have the community, we have the content. Fully co-branded, low lift, real value.

Bryan

campaigns.title: Good Investor (Tim) — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Peer-to-peer — Tim is a small-check investor himself (~$10K), so his network is people trying to figure out the same things he is. Frame it as a resource for his community, not a brand play.
campaigns.blocker: Need Tim's last name from Bryan before sending. Draft is ready to go once confirmed.
campaigns.usageNote: Bryan's direct connection. Most casual of the five pitches — keep the tone warm and personal. Get Tim's last name from Bryan first.
campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar idea — for your climate investor community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Tim,

Bryan here. Good to reconnect.

I'm putting together a webinar for people who are making small-check climate tech investments — $10K range — and want a real framework for how these deals actually play out. How exits work, what the risk profile looks like, what to pay attention to before you commit. The kind of content that usually lives behind closed doors.

Thought of you because I know your community is probably wrestling with some of these same questions. I'd love to co-host a session with you — you bring the people, we bring the framework. Nothing fancy, just a useful conversation.

Interested?

Bryan
